title: scurrula buddleioides var. heynei
intro: Scurrula buddleioides var. heynei, also known as the Heyne's scurly tree, is a plant species in the family Santalaceae. It is a hemi-parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other trees, typically those in the family Apocynaceae. The medicinal properties of Scurrula buddleioides var. heynei include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as an infusion, powder, or ointment, and its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing.
